str:Code.com:Name: Commodity Import Price Index, Individual Commodites Weighted by Ratio of Imports to Total Commodity Imports
str:Code.com:Description: This index measures the changes in the prices of a basket of goods imported into a country over time. It reflects the cost trends of imported commodities, including energy sources, raw materials, and food products. A higher index indicates increasing import prices, which may affect domestic inflation rates and the purchasing power of consumers, as well as impact the costs for businesses relying on imported goods. Weights commodities in an import index based on their share of the total value of commodity imports.

str:Code.com:Name: Commodity Import Price Index, Individual Commodites Weighted by Ratio of Imports to GDP
str:Code.com:Description: This index measures the changes in the prices of a basket of goods imported into a country over time. It reflects the cost trends of imported commodities, including energy sources, raw materials, and food products. A higher index indicates increasing import prices, which may affect domestic inflation rates and the purchasing power of consumers, as well as impact the costs for businesses relying on imported goods. Weights individual commodities in an import index based on their share of the country's GDP.

str:Code.com:Name: Commodity Export Price Index, Individual Commodites Weighted by Ratio of Exports to Total Commodity Exports
str:Code.com:Description: A measure that tracks the changes over time in the prices of a basket of goods exported by a country. This index provides insight into the trends of export prices for commodities such as oil, metals, and agricultural products. An increase in the index suggests that the prices of exported commodities are rising, potentially leading to higher revenues for exporters. A method of weighting individual commodities in an export index based on their share of the total value of commodity exports.

str:Code.com:Name: Commodity Export Price Index, Individual Commodites Weighted by Ratio of Exports to GDP
str:Code.com:Description: A measure that tracks the changes over time in the prices of a basket of goods exported by a country. This index provides insight into the trends of export prices for commodities such as oil, metals, and agricultural products. An increase in the index suggests that the prices of exported commodities are rising, potentially leading to higher revenues for exporters. A method of weighting individual commodities in an index based on the proportion of their export value relative to the country's Gross Domestic Product (GDP).

str:Code.com:Name: Commodity Net Export Price Index, Individual Commodities Weighted by Ratio of Net Exports to Total Commodity Trade
str:Code.com:Description: Represents the difference in the trends of export and import price indices for commodities. It is calculated by subtracting the Commodity Import Price Index from the Commodity Export Price Index. A positive value indicates that the prices of exports are increasing at a faster rate than the prices of imports, potentially leading to a favorable trade balance and contributing to economic growth. This weights commodities based on their net export value relative to the total value of commodity trade (exports plus imports)

str:Code.com:Name: Commodity Net Export Price Index, Individual Commodities Weighted by Ratio of Net Exports to GDP
str:Code.com:Description: Represents the difference in the trends of export and import price indices for commodities. It is calculated by subtracting the Commodity Import Price Index from the Commodity Export Price Index. A positive value indicates that the prices of exports are increasing at a faster rate than the prices of imports, potentially leading to a favorable trade balance and contributing to economic growth. This weights commodities based on their net export value (exports minus imports) relative to GDP. It focuses on commodities where the country has a trade surplus or deficit, indicating the net impact of these commodities on the economy.

str:Code.com:Name: US dollar, SDR per US dollar, Period average
str:Code.com:Description: Exchange rate indicating how many Special Drawing Rights (SDRs) are equivalent to one US dollar. This refers to the arithmetic mean (average) value of a data series calculated over a specified period, such as a month, quarter, or year. It provides a representative value for the entire period, smoothing out any short-term fluctuations.

str:Code.com:Name: Pound sterling, Domestic currency per US dollar, Period average
str:Code.com:Description: Domestic Currency per US Dollar refers to the exchange rate between a country's domestic currency and the US dollar. It indicates how much of the domestic currency is needed to buy one US dollar. This refers to the arithmetic mean (average) value of a data series calculated over a specified period, such as a month, quarter, or year. It provides a representative value for the entire period, smoothing out any short-term fluctuations.

str:Code.com:Name: الدول الهشة والمتأثرة بالنزاعات
Нестабильные и затронутые конфликтом государства

Fragile and Conflict-affected States (FCS)
États fragiles et touchés par un conflit
Estados Frágiles y Afectados por Conflictos
脆弱和受冲突影响的国家
str:Code.com:Description: This is a country grouping established by the World Bank's classification of countries in fragile and conflict-affected situations. The list consists of (i) countries with high levels of institutional and social fragility and (ii) countries affected by violent conflict, using indicators and thresholds assessed by the World Bank. It is updated annually and available publicly. Under the new IMF strategy for Fragile and Conflicted States (FCS), the Fund has adopted the methodology, thresholds, and criteria of the World Bank's Fragility and Conflict Situations for the IMF Fragile and Conflicted States. This is explained in the FCS Strategy Board paper: The IMF Strategy for Fragile and Conflict-Affected States (March 14, 2022, p. 21-22).

脆弱和受冲突影响的国家
str:Code.com:Description: This is a country grouping established by the World Bank's classification of countries in fragile and conflict-affected situations. The list consists of (i) countries with high levels of institutional and social fragility and (ii) countries affected by violent conflict, using indicators and thresholds assessed by the World Bank. It is updated annually and available publicly. Under the new IMF strategy for Fragile and Conflicted States (FCS), the Fund has adopted the methodology, thresholds, and criteria of the World Bank's Fragility and Conflict Situations for the IMF Fragile and Conflicted States. This is explained in the FCS Strategy Board paper: The IMF Strategy for Fragile and Conflict-Affected States (March 14, 2022, p. 21-22).

str:Code.com:Description: All economies covered by the dataset. Consult the dataset documentation for the specific composition of economies at each point in time.

str:Code.com:Name: Euro Area (EA)
str:Code.com:Description: Economies that are members of the Euro Area. https://ec.europa.eu/info/business-economy-euro/euro-area/what-euro-area_en

str:Code.com:Name: ASEAN
str:Code.com:Description: The Association of Southeast Asian Nations is a political and economic union of 10 states in Southeast Asia. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Member_states_of_ASEAN)

str:Code.com:Name: Southern African Development Community (SADC)
str:Code.com:Description: Member states of the Southern African Development Community (SADC): https://www.sadc.int/

str:Code.com:Name: Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa (COMESA)
str:Code.com:Description: Member states of the largest regional economic organization in Africa, the Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa (COMESA), which includes a free trade area and a customs union (launched in 2009): https://www.comesa.int/members/

str:Code.com:Name: Benelux
str:Code.com:Description: Grouping of Belgium, the Netherlands, and Luxembourg. The term "Benelux," formed from the first two letters of each country's name, originally referred to a customs union established in 1948. Today, the term is used as a generic grouping of the three countries. (Eurostat)

str:Code.com:Name: Integrity of the Monetary Policymaking Body (IA)

str:Code.com:Name: Independence and Accountability (IA)
str:Code.com:Description: Unweighted average of the sub-indices Delegation and Designation of Responsibility, Mandated Goals and Numerical Targets, Integrity of the Monetary Policymaking Body, Financial Arrangements, and Reporting and Oversight. The index is normalized to 100 for the first assessment year for each individual country. Please check the working paper "Monetary Policy Frameworks: An Index and New Evidence" for further information.

str:Code.com:Name: Policy and Operational Strategy (P0)
str:Code.com:Description: Unweighted average of the sub-indices Objectives, Numerical Targets, Tools, Policy Formulation, and Policy Implementation. The index is normalized to 100 for the first assessment year for each individual country. Please check the working paper "Monetary Policy Frameworks: An Index and New Evidence" for further information.

str:Code.com:Name: Objectives (PO)
str:Code.com:Description: The index is normalized to 100 for the first assessment year for each individual country. Please check the working paper "Monetary Policy Frameworks: An Index and New Evidence" for further information.

str:Code.com:Description: The index is normalized to 100 for the first assessment year for each individual country. Please check the working paper "Monetary Policy Frameworks: An Index and New Evidence" for further information.

str:Code.com:Name: Communications (C)
str:Code.com:Description: Unweighted average of the sub-indices Communication Cycle, Announcing and Explaining the Policy Stance, Monetary Policy Report, Publication of Data, and Stakeholder Inclusion. The index is normalized to 100 for the first assessment year for each individual country. Please check the working paper "Monetary Policy Frameworks: An Index and New Evidence" for further information.

str:Code.com:Name: Independence and Accountability, Policy and Operational Strategy, Communication
str:Code.com:Description: Unweighted average of the indices Independence and Accountability, Policy and Operational Strategy, and Communications. The index is normalized to 100 for the first assessment year for each individual country. Please check the working paper "Monetary Policy Frameworks: An Index and New Evidence" for further information.

str:Code.com:Name: Transport, Credit/Revenue, BOP: Current account
str:Code.com:Description: Transport Credit entries relate to exports of goods and services, earned income receivable, transfer income receivable and disposals of non-produced non-financial assets (the financial account in the external accounts uses the terms net acquisition of financial assets and net incurrence of liabilities rather than credit and debit). The current account covers the transactions in goods, services, earned income and transfer income for institutional sectors and between residents and non-residents.

Credit entries relate to exports of goods and services, earned income receivable, transfer income receivable and disposals of non-produced non-financial assets (the financial account in the external accounts uses the terms net acquisition of financial assets and net incurrence of liabilities rather than credit and debit). The current account covers the transactions in goods, services, earned income and transfer income for institutional sectors and between residents and non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Other modes of transport; passenger, Credit/Revenue, BOP: Current account
str:Code.com:Description: Other modes of transport; passenger Credit entries relate to exports of goods and services, earned income receivable, transfer income receivable and disposals of non-produced non-financial assets (the financial account in the external accounts uses the terms net acquisition of financial assets and net incurrence of liabilities rather than credit and debit). The current account covers the transactions in goods, services, earned income and transfer income for institutional sectors and between residents and non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: All modes of transport; passenger, Credit/Revenue, BOP: Current account
str:Code.com:Description: The conveyance of passengers using any form of transportation, including air, sea, rail, and road. Credit entries relate to exports of goods and services, earned income receivable, transfer income receivable and disposals of non-produced non-financial assets (the financial account in the external accounts uses the terms net acquisition of financial assets and net incurrence of liabilities rather than credit and debit). The current account covers the transactions in goods, services, earned income and transfer income for institutional sectors and between residents and non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Travel; personal; health-related, Credit/Revenue, BOP: Current account
str:Code.com:Description: Travel; personal; health-related Credit entries relate to exports of goods and services, earned income receivable, transfer income receivable and disposals of non-produced non-financial assets (the financial account in the external accounts uses the terms net acquisition of financial assets and net incurrence of liabilities rather than credit and debit). The current account covers the transactions in goods, services, earned income and transfer income for institutional sectors and between residents and non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Services, Credit/Revenue
str:Code.com:Description: Services results from a production activity that changes the conditions of the consuming units, or facilitates the exchange of products or financial assets. Services are not generally separate items over which ownership rights can be established and cannot generally be separated from their production. Credit entries relate to exports of goods and services, earned income receivable, transfer income receivable and disposals of non-produced non-financial assets (the financial account in the external accounts uses the terms net acquisition of financial assets and net incurrence of liabilities rather than credit and debit).

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP)
str:Code.com:Description: Gross domestic product reflects the total income earned through the production of goods and services in an economic territory during an accounting period. It can be measured in three different ways: using either the expenditure approach, the income approach, or the production approach.

str:Code.com:Name: Goods, Credit/Revenue
str:Code.com:Description: Goods are physical, produced objects, over which ownership rights can be established and whose ownership can be transferred from one institutional unit to another by engaging in transactions on markets. Credit entries relate to exports of goods and services, earned income receivable, transfer income receivable and disposals of non-produced non-financial assets (the financial account in the external accounts uses the terms net acquisition of financial assets and net incurrence of liabilities rather than credit and debit).

str:Code.com:Name: WEO Oil Price
str:Code.com:Description: Oil price assumption used for the World Economic Outlook (WEO) projections, US$ per barrel. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published.

str:Code.com:Name: Other metals
str:Code.com:Description: Other Metals Index, 2016 = 100. This indicator reflects price movements in metals not included in the Base Metals or Precious Metals indices. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published.

str:Code.com:Name: Metals, excluding others
str:Code.com:Description: Metals Price Index, 2016 = 100, excluding the Other Metals Index. This indicator includes the Base Metals and Precious Metals indices. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published.

str:Code.com:Name: Other food and beverage
str:Code.com:Description: Other Food and Beverage Price Index, 2016 = 100. This indicator reflects price movements in food and beverages not included in the main Food and Beverage indices. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published.

str:Code.com:Name: Food and beverage, excluding others
str:Code.com:Description: Food and Beverage Price Index, 2016 = 100, excluding the Other Food and Beverage Index. This indicator includes the main Food and Beverage indices. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published.

str:Code.com:Name: Other fertilizers
str:Code.com:Description: Other Fertilizers Index, 2016 = 100. This indicator reflects price movements in fertilizers not included in the main Fertilizer index (DAP, Potash, Urea). This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published.

str:Code.com:Name: Fertilizers, excluding others
str:Code.com:Description: Fertilizers Price Index, 2016 = 100, excluding the Other Fertilizers Index. This indicator includes DAP, Potash, and Urea prices. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published.

str:Code.com:Name: Other raw materials
str:Code.com:Description: Other Raw Materials Index, 2016 = 100. This indicator reflects price movements in agricultural raw materials not included in the main Agricultural Raw Materials Index (Timber, Cotton, Wool, Rubber, Hides). This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published.

str:Code.com:Name: Raw materials, excluding others
str:Code.com:Description: Raw Materials Price Index, 2016 = 100, excluding the Other Raw Materials Index. This indicator includes Timber, Cotton, Wool, Rubber, and Hides prices. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published.

Extensive Margin, Theil index
str:Code.com:Description: Extensive Margin Index. A Theil index can be interpreted as a measure of redundancy, lack of diversity, isolation, segregation, inequality, non-randomness, and compressibility. In the context of diversification, the higher the index the less diversification. A statistical measure that expresses the relative change in a variable in the current period compared to the index reference period.

str:Code.com:Name: Export Diversification, Theil index
str:Code.com:Description: Export Diversification Index. A Theil index can be interpreted as a measure of redundancy, lack of diversity, isolation, segregation, inequality, non-randomness, and compressibility. In the context of diversification, the higher the index the less diversification. A statistical measure that expresses the relative change in a variable in the current period compared to the index reference period.

str:Code.com:Name: Intensive Margin, Theil index
str:Code.com:Description: Intensive Margin Index. A Theil index can be interpreted as a measure of redundancy, lack of diversity, isolation, segregation, inequality, non-randomness, and compressibility. In the context of diversification, the higher the index the less diversification. A statistical measure that expresses the relative change in a variable in the current period compared to the index reference period.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Monetary Policy Frameworks Toolkit (MPFT) Type of Transformation

str:Code.com:Name: Index
str:Code.com:Description: A statistical measure that expresses the relative change in a variable in the current period compared to the index reference period.

str:Code.com:Name: Index, Percent change
str:Code.com:Description: A statistical measure that expresses the relative change in a variable in the current period compared to the index reference period. This measures the relative change in a value over time, expressed as a percentage. It is calculated as the difference between the new value and the old value, divided by the old value, and multiplied by 100. 1

Economías de Mercados Emergentes y Países en Desarrollo por Financiamiento Externo: Deudores Netos con Atrasos y/o Refinanciaciones

str:Code.com:Name: الاتحاد الأوروبي
Европейский Союз (ЕС)
欧州連合（EU）
European Union (EU)
Union européenne (UE)
Unión Europea (UE)
欧洲联盟 (欧盟)
str:Code.com:Description: Economies that are members of the European Union. Umbrella term referring to a three "pillar" construction encompassing the European Community (EC), Common Foreign and Security Policy, and Justice and Home Affairs.

str:Code.com:Name: الدول الهشة والمتأثرة بالنزاعات
Нестабильные и затронутые конфликтом государства
脆弱で紛争の影響を受けている国家
Fragile and Conflict-affected States (FCS)
États fragiles et touchés par un conflit
Estados Frágiles y Afectados por Conflictos
脆弱和受冲突影响的国家
str:Code.com:Description: This is a country grouping established by the World Bank's classification of countries in fragile and conflict-affected situations. The list consists of (i) countries with high levels of institutional and social fragility and (ii) countries affected by violent conflict, using indicators and thresholds assessed by the World Bank. It is updated annually and available publicly. Under the new IMF strategy for Fragile and Conflicted States (FCS), the Fund has adopted the methodology, thresholds, and criteria of the World Bank's Fragility and Conflict Situations for the IMF Fragile and Conflicted States. This is explained in the FCS Strategy Board paper: The IMF Strategy for Fragile and Conflict-Affected States (March 14, 2022, p. 21-22).

str:Code.com:Name: Benelux
str:Code.com:Description: Grouping of Belgium, the Netherlands, and Luxembourg. The term "Benelux," formed from the first two letters of each country's name, originally referred to a customs union established in 1948. Today, the term is used as a generic grouping of the three countries. (Eurostat)

str:Code.com:Name: South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands

str:Codelist.com:Name: International Trade in Services (ITS) Type of Transformation

str:Code.com:Name: Services Export Density Index
str:Code.com:Description: A country's likelihood to export services s in the future.

str:Code.com:Name: Herfindahl index
str:Code.com:Description: The Herfindahl-Hirschman Index (HHI) is a measure used in economics to gauge market concentration, indicating the level of competition within an industry. It's calculated by squaring the market share of each firm competing in a market and then summing the resulting numbers.

str:Code.com:Name: Natural Log Difference between 2012 and 2000 for a given country's total service exports
str:Code.com:Description: Natural Log Difference between 2012 and 2000 for a given country's total service exports

str:Code.com:Name: Natural log of a country's service export in 2000
str:Code.com:Description: Natural log of a country's service export in 2000

str:Code.com:Name: ln (GDP per capita)
str:Code.com:Description: Natural log. GDP per capita is gross domestic product divided by midyear population. GDP is the sum of gross value added by all resident producers in the economy plus any product taxes and minus any subsidies not included in the value of the products. It is calculated without making deductions for depreciation of fabricated assets or for depletion and degradation of natural resources. Data are in constant 2005 U.S. dollars.

str:Code.com:Name: ln (Service Exports)
str:Code.com:Description: Natual log of total service exports

str:Code.com:Name: ln (service export sophistication)
str:Code.com:Description: Natural log of sophistication in exporting services

str:Code.com:Name: Per capita, US Dollar
str:Code.com:Description: Per capita is a Latin term that means "for each head" or "per person." It is often used in economic and statistical analysis to compare data across populations of different sizes. When a figure is given per capita, it means the average amount per person. Currency of the United States of America.

str:Code.com:Name: Services Export Path Index
str:Code.com:Description: A services's association to other services.

str:Code.com:Name: Services Export Revealed Comparative Advantage Index
str:Code.com:Description: Revealed Comparative Advantage (degree of specialization) for a country to export a specific service sector.

str:Code.com:Name: Share of Goods Exports in Total Exports, Period Average (end-of-period)
str:Code.com:Description: Share of Goods Exports in Total Exports, Period Average (end-of-period)

str:Code.com:Name: Share of Service Exports in GDP
str:Code.com:Description: Percent of total export of services for a country as a share of country GDP. More formally, Total Service Exports (current US$) / GDP (current US$)

str:Code.com:Name: Share of Service Exports in Total Exports
str:Code.com:Description: Percent of total export basket accounted by Service Exports. More formally, Total Service Exports (current US$) / Total Exports of Goods and Services (current US$)

str:Code.com:Name: Share of Service Exports in Total Exports, Period Average (end-of-period)
str:Code.com:Description: Average annual Percent of Service Exports in Regional Aggregate Total Export Basket

str:Code.com:Name: Share of Service Exports in World Service Exports
str:Code.com:Description: Percent of total export basket in world service exports. More formally, Total Service Exports in Country (current US$) / Total Exports of Services Exports in World (current US$)

str:Code.com:Name: Share of Service Exports in World Service Exports, Diversity
str:Code.com:Description: Percent of total export basket in world service exports. More formally, Total Service Exports in Country (current US$) / Total Exports of Services Exports in World (current US$)

str:Code.com:Name: Share of Exports in Country's Service Export Basket
str:Code.com:Description: Share of Exports in Country's Service Export Basket

str:Code.com:Name: Share of Exports in Country's Service Export Basket, 1 Digit Level, Percent
str:Code.com:Description: Share of Exports in Country's Service Export Basket, 1 Digit Level, Percent

str:Code.com:Name: Share of Exports in Country's Service Export Basket, 2 Digit Level, Percent
str:Code.com:Description: Share of Exports in Country's Service Export Basket, 2 Digit Level, Percent

str:Code.com:Name: Year-over-year (YOY) percent change, period average
str:Code.com:Description: The average percentage change over two consecutive years.

str:Code.com:Name: Year-over-year (YOY) percent change, Index
str:Code.com:Description: The percentage change over two consecutive years. Index is used here as the unit of measure for values that result from an index computation.

str:Code.com:Name: Year-over-year (YOY) percent change
str:Code.com:Description: The percentage change over two consecutive years.

str:Code.com:Name: Number
str:Code.com:Description: A number is a mathematical object used to count, measure, and label.

str:Code.com:Name: US Dollar
str:Code.com:Description: Currency of the United States of America.

str:Code.com:Name: Export quality, 95 percent interval - lower bound, Percent
str:Code.com:Description: Export quality The lower bound of a 95 percent confidence interval, representing the lower limit within which a parameter is likely to fall. A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Export quality, 95 percent interval - upper bound, Percent
str:Code.com:Description: Export quality The upper bound of a 95 percent confidence interval, representing the upper limit within which a parameter is likely to fall. A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Export quality index
str:Code.com:Description: Export quality index. A statistical measure that expresses the relative change in a variable over time.

str:Code.com:Name: Exporter real gross domestic product, Per capita, US dollar
str:Code.com:Description: Exporter real gross domestic product Per capita is a Latin term that means "for each head" or "per person." It is often used in economic and statistical analysis to compare data across populations of different sizes. When a figure is given per capita, it means the average amount per person. For example, GDP per capita is a measure of a country's economic output per person.

str:Code.com:Name: Export unit values, 95 percent interval - lower bound, Percent
str:Code.com:Description: Export unit values The lower bound of a 95 percent confidence interval, representing the lower limit within which a parameter is likely to fall. A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Export unit values, 95 percent interval - upper bound, Percent
str:Code.com:Description: Export unit values The upper bound of a 95 percent confidence interval, representing the upper limit within which a parameter is likely to fall. A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Import unit values, Mean, US dollar
str:Code.com:Description: Import unit values The mean is a measure of central tendency, calculated by summing all values in a dataset and dividing by the number of values.

str:Code.com:Name: Export unit values, US dollar
str:Code.com:Description: Export unit values

str:Code.com:Name: Import quality, Mean, US dollar
str:Code.com:Description: Import quality The mean is a measure of central tendency, calculated by summing all values in a dataset and dividing by the number of values.

str:Code.com:Name: الدول الهشة والمتأثرة بالنزاعات
Нестабильные и затронутые конфликтом государства

Fragile and Conflict-affected States (FCS)
États fragiles et touchés par un conflit
Estados Frágiles y Afectados por Conflictos
脆弱和受冲突影响的国家
str:Code.com:Description: This is a country grouping established by the World Bank's classification of countries in fragile and conflict-affected situations. The list consists of (i) countries with high levels of institutional and social fragility and (ii) countries affected by violent conflict, using indicators and thresholds assessed by the World Bank. It is updated annually and available publicly. Under the new IMF strategy for Fragile and Conflicted States (FCS), the Fund has adopted the methodology, thresholds, and criteria of the World Bank's Fragility and Conflict Situations for the IMF Fragile and Conflicted States. This is explained in the FCS Strategy Board paper: The IMF Strategy for Fragile and Conflict-Affected States (March 14, 2022, p. 21-22).

str:Code.com:Description: All economies covered by the dataset. Consult the dataset documentation for the specific composition of economies at each point in time.

str:Code.com:Name: Euro Area (EA)
str:Code.com:Description: Economies that are members of the Euro Area. https://ec.europa.eu/info/business-economy-euro/euro-area/what-euro-area_en

str:Code.com:Name: ASEAN
str:Code.com:Description: The Association of Southeast Asian Nations is a political and economic union of 10 states in Southeast Asia. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Member_states_of_ASEAN)

str:Code.com:Name: Southern African Development Community (SADC)
str:Code.com:Description: Member states of the Southern African Development Community (SADC): https://www.sadc.int/

str:Code.com:Name: Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa (COMESA)
str:Code.com:Description: Member states of the largest regional economic organization in Africa, the Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa (COMESA), which includes a free trade area and a customs union (launched in 2009): https://www.comesa.int/members/

str:Code.com:Name: Benelux
str:Code.com:Description: Grouping of Belgium, the Netherlands, and Luxembourg. The term "Benelux," formed from the first two letters of each country's name, originally referred to a customs union established in 1948. Today, the term is used as a generic grouping of the three countries. (Eurostat)

str:Code.com:Name: South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands
Unemployment rate
str:Code.com:Description: The percentage of the labor force that is unemployed and actively seeking employment. It is calculated as the number of unemployed persons divided by the total labor force. A measure that expresses the frequency of occurrence of an event or phenomenon in relation to another quantity.

str:Code.com:Name: Coal, South Africa, Export price, US dollars per metric tonne
str:Code.com:Description: Coal, South African export price, US$ per metric ton. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published. The export price of a commodity. Price or cost in US dollars per metric tonne.

str:Code.com:Name: External debt: total debt service, amortization, US dollar
str:Code.com:Description: Total external debt amortization charges reflect the amortization (interest and regular payments) charges related to gross external debt during the accounting period. Gross external debt reflects liabilities that require payment(s) of interest and/or principal by the debtor at some point(s) in the future and that are owed to non-residents by residents of an economy.

str:Code.com:Name: External debt: total debt service, interest, US dollar
str:Code.com:Description: Total external debt interest charges reflect the interest payments related to gross external debt during the accounting period. Gross external debt reflects liabilities that require payment(s) of interest and/or principal by the debtor at some point(s) in the future and that are owed to non-residents by residents of an economy.

str:Code.com:Name: External debt: total debt service, amortization, Percent of GDP
str:Code.com:Description: Total external debt amortization charges reflect the amortization (interest and regular payments) charges related to gross external debt during the accounting period. Gross external debt reflects liabilities that require payment(s) of interest and/or principal by the debtor at some point(s) in the future and that are owed to non-residents by residents of an economy. Value expressed as a percent of the Gross Domestic Product (GDP) of a country. A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of goods and services, US dollar
str:Code.com:Description: The export (sale or barter) of physical, produced objects (goods) and services over which ownership rights can be established and whose ownership can be transferred from one institutional unit to another by engaging in transactions on markets by residents in one territory to residents in another territory. Exports reflect a change in the economic ownership of goods from residents of the compiling economy to non-residents, irrespective of physical movement of goods across national borders. The f.o.b. price (free on board price) of exports and imports of goods is the market value of the goods at the point of uniform valuation, (the customs frontier of the economy from which they are exported). It is equal to the c.i.f. price less the costs of transportation and insurance charges, between the customs frontier of the exporting (importing) country and that of the importing (exporting) country. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to perserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published.

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of goods and services, US dollar
str:Code.com:Description: Total current and capital account balances (credit less debit)

str:Code.com:Name: External debt: total debt service, US dollar
str:Code.com:Description: Total external debt service charges reflect the amortization (interest and regular payments) and principle payments related to gross external debt during the accounting period. Gross external debt reflects liabilities that require payment(s) of interest and/or principal by the debtor at some point(s) in the future and that are owed to non-residents by residents of an economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Terms of trade of goods and services, percent change
str:Code.com:Description: The percentage change in the terms of trade of goods and services over a specific period. A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: External debt: total debt service, interest, Percent of GDP
str:Code.com:Description: Total external debt interest charges reflect the interest payments related to gross external debt during the accounting period. Gross external debt reflects liabilities that require payment(s) of interest and/or principal by the debtor at some point(s) in the future and that are owed to non-residents by residents of an economy. Value expressed as a percent of the Gross Domestic Product (GDP) of a country. A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP), Constant prices, Percent
str:Code.com:Description: Gross domestic product reflects the total income earned through the production of goods and services in an economic territory during an accounting period. It can be measured in three different ways: using either the expenditure approach, the income approach, or the production approach. Values that have had the effect of price changes removed, expressed as time series in constant prices (fixed base) or chain-linked volume terms, or as non-time series in previous year's prices A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP), Current prices, US dollar
str:Code.com:Description: Gross domestic product reflects the total income earned through the production of goods and services in an economic territory during an accounting period. It can be measured in three different ways: using either the expenditure approach, the income approach, or the production approach. Values expressed without any adjustment for price changes

str:Code.com:Name: Tea, Kenyan, Unit prices, US cents per kilogram
str:Code.com:Description: Tea, Mombasa, Kenya, Auction Price, US cents per kilogram, From July 1998,Kenya auctions, Best Pekoe Fannings. Prior, London auctions, c.i.f. U.K. warehouses. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published. The unitary price of an item or commodity. Price or cost in US cents per kilogram.

str:Code.com:Name: External debt: total debt service, Percent of GDP
str:Code.com:Description: Total external debt service charges reflect the amortization (interest and regular payments) and principle payments related to gross external debt during the accounting period. Gross external debt reflects liabilities that require payment(s) of interest and/or principal by the debtor at some point(s) in the future and that are owed to non-residents by residents of an economy. Value expressed as a percent of the Gross Domestic Product (GDP) of a country. A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: External debt, US dollar
str:Code.com:Description: Gross external debt reflects liabilities that require payment(s) of interest and/or principal by the debtor at some point(s) in the future and that are owed to non-residents by residents of an economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP), Price deflator, Index
str:Code.com:Description: Gross domestic product reflects the total income earned through the production of goods and services in an economic territory during an accounting period. It can be measured in three different ways: using either the expenditure approach, the income approach, or the production approach. An economic indicator used to adjust nominal economic data series for changes in relevant price levels. Price deflators can be explicit, such as producer or consumer price indices, or implicit, such as the GDP deflator derived by dividing nominal GDP by GDP in volume terms. A statistical measure that expresses the relative change in a variable over time.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP), Constant prices, Percent change
str:Code.com:Description: Gross domestic product reflects the total income earned through the production of goods and services in an economic territory during an accounting period. It can be measured in three different ways: using either the expenditure approach, the income approach, or the production approach. Values that have had the effect of price changes removed, expressed as time series in constant prices (fixed base) or chain-linked volume terms, or as non-time series in previous year's prices This measures the relative change in a value over time, expressed as a percentage. It is calculated as the difference between the new value and the old value, divided by the old value, and multiplied by 100. 1 A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Terms of trade of goods, percent change
str:Code.com:Description: The percentage change in the terms of trade of goods over a specific period. A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP), Constant prices, Domestic currency
str:Code.com:Description: Gross domestic product reflects the total income earned through the production of goods and services in an economic territory during an accounting period. It can be measured in three different ways: using either the expenditure approach, the income approach, or the production approach. Values that have had the effect of price changes removed, expressed as time series in constant prices (fixed base) or chain-linked volume terms, or as non-time series in previous year's prices Currency issued by the country of origin, used internally. Used to specify what Unit of Measure the country used to provide data to the IMF.�

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP), Current prices, Domestic currency
str:Code.com:Description: Gross domestic product reflects the total income earned through the production of goods and services in an economic territory during an accounting period. It can be measured in three different ways: using either the expenditure approach, the income approach, or the production approach. Values expressed without any adjustment for price changes Currency issued by the country of origin, used internally. Used to specify what Unit of Measure the country used to provide data to the IMF.�

str:Code.com:Name: Gross national savings, Percent of GDP
str:Code.com:Description: Gross national saving represents the total amount of income earned by residents of an economic territory (including households, nonprofit institutions serving housholds, corporations, government entities) that is not consumed during a specific period. Value expressed as a percent of the Gross Domestic Product (GDP) of a country. A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Sunflower oil, Export price, US dollars per metric tonne
str:Code.com:Description: Sunflower oil, Sunflower Oil, US export price from Gulf of Mexico, US$ per metric ton. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP), Purchasing power parity (PPP) international dollar, percent of world GDP, Percent, ICP benchmarks 2017-2021
str:Code.com:Description: Gross domestic product reflects the total income earned through the production of goods and services in an economic territory during an accounting period. It can be measured in three different ways: using either the expenditure approach, the income approach, or the production approach. Share of global GDP attributed to a country, adjusted for purchasing power parity. A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100. ICP benchmarks 2017-2021

str:Code.com:Name: External debt, Percent of GDP
str:Code.com:Description: Gross external debt reflects liabilities that require payment(s) of interest and/or principal by the debtor at some point(s) in the future and that are owed to non-residents by residents of an economy. Value expressed as a percent of the Gross Domestic Product (GDP) of a country. A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP), Per capita, purchasing power parity (PPP) international dollar, ICP benchmarks 2017-2021
str:Code.com:Description: Gross domestic product reflects the total income earned through the production of goods and services in an economic territory during an accounting period. It can be measured in three different ways: using either the expenditure approach, the income approach, or the production approach. Average income or economic output per person adjusted for cost-of-living differences between countries, expressed in international dollars. ICP benchmarks 2017-2021

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP), Current prices, Purchasing power parity (PPP) international dollar, ICP benchmarks 2017-2021
str:Code.com:Description: Gross domestic product reflects the total income earned through the production of goods and services in an economic territory during an accounting period. It can be measured in three different ways: using either the expenditure approach, the income approach, or the production approach. Values expressed without any adjustment for price changes Rates of currency conversion that equalize purchasing power by eliminating price level differences between countries. ICP benchmarks 2017-2021

str:Code.com:Name: External debt: total debt service, amortization, Percent of exports of goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Total external debt amortization charges reflect the amortization (interest and regular payments) charges related to gross external debt during the accounting period. Gross external debt reflects liabilities that require payment(s) of interest and/or principal by the debtor at some point(s) in the future and that are owed to non-residents by residents of an economy. Base concept expressed as a ratio to Exports of Goods and Services (BXGS). A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP), Current prices, Per capita, US dollar
str:Code.com:Description: Gross domestic product reflects the total income earned through the production of goods and services in an economic territory during an accounting period. It can be measured in three different ways: using either the expenditure approach, the income approach, or the production approach. Values expressed without any adjustment for price changes Per capita is a Latin term that means "for each head" or "per person." It is often used in economic and statistical analysis to compare data across populations of different sizes. When a figure is given per capita, it means the average amount per person. For example, GDP per capita is a measure of a country's economic output per person.

str:Code.com:Name: Revenue, General government, Percent of GDP
str:Code.com:Description: Revenue refers to the total inflow of resources, typically in the form of taxes, fees, charges, grants, and other income, received by a government entity during a specific period. It represents the funds generated by the government through its various activities and sources, including taxation, sales of goods or services, investment income, and transfers from other entities. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments. Value expressed as a percent of the Gross Domestic Product (GDP) of a country. A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Trade of goods and services, Volume, Percent change
str:Code.com:Description: Trade of goods and services The quantity of goods or services produced or traded, often measured in terms of physical units or monetary value. This measures the relative change in a value over time, expressed as a percentage. It is calculated as the difference between the new value and the old value, divided by the old value, and multiplied by 100. 1 A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Current account balance (credit less debit), US dollar
str:Code.com:Description: The current account balance is the balance of current transactions (transactions in goods and services, earned income and transfer income) between residents and non-residents. The term current account balance is used in the external accounts and is expressed from the perspective of resident units. The term current external balance is used in the national accounts and is expressed from the perspective of the non-resident units, and therefore with the opposite sign.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Net (assets minus liabilities), US dollar
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. The net value obtained by subtracting total liabilities from total assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Expenditure, General government, Percent of GDP
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditure refers to the total outflow of resources, including payments, purchases, and transfers, made by a government entity during a specific period to fulfill its various functions and obligations. It encompasses spending on goods and services, wages and salaries, social benefits, debt servicing, infrastructure investments, and other government programs and activities. Expenditure represents the utilization of government funds to provide public services, maintain infrastructure, implement policies, and meet financial obligations. Monitoring expenditure patterns and allocations provides insight into government priorities, policy objectives, and fiscal sustainability. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments. Value expressed as a percent of the Gross Domestic Product (GDP) of a country. A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross debt, General government, Domestic currency
str:Code.com:Description: Gross debt is the sum of all liabilities that are debt instruments. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments. Currency issued by the country of origin, used internally. Used to specify what Unit of Measure the country used to provide data to the IMF.�

str:Code.com:Name: Net debt, General government, Domestic currency
str:Code.com:Description: Net debt is the sum of all liabilities less financial assets that are debt instruments. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments. Currency issued by the country of origin, used internally. Used to specify what Unit of Measure the country used to provide data to the IMF.�

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP), Current prices, Fiscal year, Domestic currency
str:Code.com:Description: Gross domestic product reflects the total income earned through the production of goods and services in an economic territory during an accounting period. It can be measured in three different ways: using either the expenditure approach, the income approach, or the production approach. Values expressed without any adjustment for price changes A 12-month period that a company or government uses for accounting purposes. The fiscal year may or may not align with the calendar year. For example, a company's fiscal year could start on July 1st and end on June 30th of the following year. Currency issued by the country of origin, used internally. Used to specify what Unit of Measure the country used to provide data to the IMF.�

str:Code.com:Name: Expenditure, General government, Domestic currency
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditure refers to the total outflow of resources, including payments, purchases, and transfers, made by a government entity during a specific period to fulfill its various functions and obligations. It encompasses spending on goods and services, wages and salaries, social benefits, debt servicing, infrastructure investments, and other government programs and activities. Expenditure represents the utilization of government funds to provide public services, maintain infrastructure, implement policies, and meet financial obligations. Monitoring expenditure patterns and allocations provides insight into government priorities, policy objectives, and fiscal sustainability. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments. Currency issued by the country of origin, used internally. Used to specify what Unit of Measure the country used to provide data to the IMF.�

str:Code.com:Name: External debt, Percent of exports of goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Gross external debt reflects liabilities that require payment(s) of interest and/or principal by the debtor at some point(s) in the future and that are owed to non-residents by residents of an economy. Base concept expressed as a ratio to Exports of Goods and Services (BXGS). A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Net (assets minus liabilities), US dollar
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship. The net value obtained by subtracting total liabilities from total assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of goods and services, Volume, Free on board (FOB), Percent change
str:Code.com:Description: Exports of goods and services reflect the export of goods reflect changes in the economic ownership of goods from residents of the compiling economy to non-residents, irrespective of physical movement of goods across national borders. Exports of services provided by residents to non-residents. The quantity of goods or services produced or traded, often measured in terms of physical units or monetary value. The f.o.b. price (free on board price) of exports and imports of goods is the market value of the goods at the point of uniform valuation, (the customs frontier of the economy from which they are exported). It is equal to the c.i.f. price less the costs of transportation and insurance charges, between the customs frontier of the exporting (importing) country and that of the importing (exporting) country. This measures the relative change in a value over time, expressed as a percentage. It is calculated as the difference between the new value and the old value, divided by the old value, and multiplied by 100. 1 A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Net (assets minus liabilities), US dollar
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. The net value obtained by subtracting total liabilities from total assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP), Constant prices, Per capita, purchasing power parity (PPP) international dollar, ICP benchmark 2021
str:Code.com:Description: Gross domestic product reflects the total income earned through the production of goods and services in an economic territory during an accounting period. It can be measured in three different ways: using either the expenditure approach, the income approach, or the production approach. Values that have had the effect of price changes removed, expressed as time series in constant prices (fixed base) or chain-linked volume terms, or as non-time series in previous year's prices Average income or economic output per person adjusted for cost-of-living differences between countries, expressed in international dollars. ICP benchmark 2021

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of manufactures, Price deflator, Free on board (FOB), Index
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of manufactured goods sold by a country to foreign buyers. An economic indicator used to adjust nominal economic data series for changes in relevant price levels. Price deflators can be explicit, such as producer or consumer price indices, or implicit, such as the GDP deflator derived by dividing nominal GDP by GDP in volume terms. The f.o.b. price (free on board price) of exports and imports of goods is the market value of the goods at the point of uniform valuation, (the customs frontier of the economy from which they are exported). It is equal to the c.i.f. price less the costs of transportation and insurance charges, between the customs frontier of the exporting (importing) country and that of the importing (exporting) country. A statistical measure that expresses the relative change in a variable over time.

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of goods and services, Volume, Cost insurance freight (CIF), Percent change
str:Code.com:Description: Imports of goods and services reflect the change in the economic ownership of goods from non-residents to residents of the compiling economy, irrespective of physical movement of goods across national borders. Imports of services reflect services provided by non-residents to residents The quantity of goods or services produced or traded, often measured in terms of physical units or monetary value. The c.i.f. price (i.e. cost, insurance and freight price) is the price of a good delivered at the frontier of the importing country, including any insurance and freight charges incurred to that point, or the price of a service delivered to a resident, before the payment of any import duties or other taxes on imports or trade and transport margins within the country. This measures the relative change in a value over time, expressed as a percentage. It is calculated as the difference between the new value and the old value, divided by the old value, and multiplied by 100. 1 A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

A commodity price index is a measurement tool that tracks the average price changes of a basket of commodities over time. These commodities can include various raw materials or primary agricultural products A statistical measure that expresses the relative change in a variable over time.

str:Code.com:Name: Net lending (+) / net borrowing (-), General government, Domestic currency
str:Code.com:Description: Net lending is defined as the difference between changes in net worth due to saving and capital transfers and net acquisitions of non-financial assets (acquisitions less disposals of non-financial assets, less depreciation). If the amount is negative, it represents net borrowing. In the external accounts, it represents the sum of the balances on the current and capital accounts. This is conceptually equal to the net balance of the financial account. If the balance is positive, it is called net lending, implying that in net terms, the economy supplies funds to the rest of the world. If the balance is negative, it is called net borrowing, implying that in net terms, the economy receives funds from the rest of the world. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments. Currency issued by the country of origin, used internally. Used to specify what Unit of Measure the country used to provide data to the IMF.�

str:Code.com:Name: Change in reserve assets, Net (assets minus liabilities), US dollar
str:Code.com:Description: The difference in the value of reserve assets between two points in time, reflecting additions or withdrawals. The net value obtained by subtracting total liabilities from total assets.

The f.o.b. price (free on board price) of exports and imports of goods is the market value of the goods at the point of uniform valuation, (the customs frontier of the economy from which they are exported). It is equal to the c.i.f. price less the costs of transportation and insurance charges, between the customs frontier of the exporting (importing) country and that of the importing (exporting) country. str:Code.com:Name: All Items, Consumer price index (CPI), Period average
str:Code.com:Description: All items in the context of the consumer price index indicates that the index includes prices for all items included in the consumer price index basket. This includes prices related to food, beverages, clothing, housing, health, transport, communications, recreation, education, restaurants and hotels and other miscellanoues goods and services. The Consumer Price Index (CPI) is an index of the prices of consumption goods and services, as compared to a certain reference period. This refers to the arithmetic mean (average) value of a data series calculated over a specified period, such as a month, quarter, or year. It provides a representative value for the entire period, smoothing out any short-term fluctuations. A statistical measure that expresses the relative change in a variable over time.

str:Code.com:Name: Primary net lending (+) / net borrowing (-), General government, Percent of GDP
str:Code.com:Description: Primary net lending/borrowing refers to the difference between a government's total revenue (excluding borrowing) and its total expenditures (excluding debt repayments and interest payments). It represents the government's fiscal position, indicating whether it is running a surplus (positive value) or a deficit (negative value) before considering the impact of debt servicing costs. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments. Value expressed as a percent of the Gross Domestic Product (GDP) of a country. This includes prices related to food, beverages, clothing, housing, health, transport, communications, recreation, education, restaurants and hotels and other miscellanoues goods and services. The Consumer Price Index (CPI) is an index of the prices of consumption goods and services, as compared to a certain reference period. Refers to the end of any specific period, such as a quarter, month, or week. It marks the point at which economic or financial data or indexes are aggregated and/or analyzed. A statistical measure that expresses the relative change in a variable over time.

str:Code.com:Name: Primary net lending (+) / net borrowing (-), General government, Domestic currency
str:Code.com:Description: Primary net lending/borrowing refers to the difference between a government's total revenue (excluding borrowing) and its total expenditures (excluding debt repayments and interest payments). It represents the government's fiscal position, indicating whether it is running a surplus (positive value) or a deficit (negative value) before considering the impact of debt servicing costs. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments. Currency issued by the country of origin, used internally. Used to specify what Unit of Measure the country used to provide data to the IMF.�

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of goods, Volume, Free on board (FOB), Percent change
str:Code.com:Description: Exports of goods reflects the change in the economic ownership of goods from residents of the compiling economy to non-residents, irrespective of physical movement of goods across national borders. The quantity of goods or services produced or traded, often measured in terms of physical units or monetary value. The f.o.b. price (free on board price) of exports and imports of goods is the market value of the goods at the point of uniform valuation, (the customs frontier of the economy from which they are exported). It is equal to the c.i.f. price less the costs of transportation and insurance charges, between the customs frontier of the exporting (importing) country and that of the importing (exporting) country. This measures the relative change in a value over time, expressed as a percentage. It is calculated as the difference between the new value and the old value, divided by the old value, and multiplied by 100. 1 A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of goods, Volume, Cost insurance freight (CIF), Percent change
str:Code.com:Description: Imports of goods reflects the change in the economic ownership of goods from non-residents to residents of the compiling economy, irrespective of physical movement of goods across national borders. The quantity of goods or services produced or traded, often measured in terms of physical units or monetary value. The c.i.f. price (i.e. cost, insurance and freight price) is the price of a good delivered at the frontier of the importing country, including any insurance and freight charges incurred to that point, or the price of a service delivered to a resident, before the payment of any import duties or other taxes on imports or trade and transport margins within the country. This measures the relative change in a value over time, expressed as a percentage. It is calculated as the difference between the new value and the old value, divided by the old value, and multiplied by 100. 1 A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Softwood, Commodity price index
str:Code.com:Description: Softwood Index, 2016 = 100, includes Softwood Sawn and Softwood Logs Price Indices. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published. A commodity price index is a measurement tool that tracks the average price changes of a basket of commodities over time. These commodities can include various raw materials or primary agricultural products A statistical measure that expresses the relative change in a variable over time.

str:Code.com:Name: Hardwood, Commodity price index
str:Code.com:Description: Hardwood Price Index, 2016 = 100, includes Hardwood Logs and Hardwood Sawn Price Indices. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published. A commodity price index is a measurement tool that tracks the average price changes of a basket of commodities over time. These commodities can include various raw materials or primary agricultural products A statistical measure that expresses the relative change in a variable over time.

str:Code.com:Name: All Items, Consumer price index (CPI), Period average, percent change
str:Code.com:Description: All items in the context of the consumer price index indicates that the index includes prices for all items included in the consumer price index basket. This includes prices related to food, beverages, clothing, housing, health, transport, communications, recreation, education, restaurants and hotels and other miscellanoues goods and services. The Consumer Price Index (CPI) is an index of the prices of consumption goods and services, as compared to a certain reference period. This calculates the change relative to the average value over the entire period, not just the starting point. This is often used when the data fluctuates significantly within the period, and the average provides a more representative value for comparison. A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Description: Meat Price Index, 2016 = 100, includes Beef, Lamb, Swine (pork), and Poultry Price Indices. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published. A commodity price index is a measurement tool that tracks the average price changes of a basket of commodities over time. These commodities can include various raw materials or primary agricultural products A statistical measure that expresses the relative change in a variable over time.

str:Code.com:Description: Food and Beverage Price Index, 2016 = 100, includes Food and Beverage Price Indices. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. A commodity price index is a measurement tool that tracks the average price changes of a basket of commodities over time. These commodities can include various raw materials or primary agricultural products A statistical measure that expresses the relative change in a variable over time.

str:Code.com:Name: All Items, Consumer price index (CPI), End-of-period (EoP), percent change
str:Code.com:Description: All items in the context of the consumer price index indicates that the index includes prices for all items included in the consumer price index basket. This includes prices related to food, beverages, clothing, housing, health, transport, communications, recreation, education, restaurants and hotels and other miscellanoues goods and services. The Consumer Price Index (CPI) is an index of the prices of consumption goods and services, as compared to a certain reference period. This focuses on the change between the value at the beginning of a period and the value at the end of that period. The period could be a day, month, quarter, year, etc. This is often used to track changes in financial or economic indicators over specific periods. A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Population, Persons for countries / Index for country groups
str:Code.com:Description: The total number of people living in a specific area or country. Persons for countries / Index for country groups

str:Code.com:Name: Rate, Domestic currency per international dollar in PPP terms, ICP benchmarks 2017-2021
str:Code.com:Description: A measure that expresses the frequency of occurrence of an event or phenomenon in relation to another quantity. Purchasing power parities (PPPs) are the rates of currency conversion that try to equalize the purchasing power of different currencies, by eliminating the differences in price levels between countries. ICP benchmarks 2017-2021
